# Connect OpenAI

Use OpenAI here, or the matching key and URL from another OpenAI-compatible provider.

> Security: Use disposable test accounts and non-personal sample data. Cover cookies, API keys, emails, names, and identifiers with solid opaque blocks before saving. Never blur a secret and never place an unredacted source image in the repository. Inspect the final image at full resolution and remove metadata before adding it.

## 1. Create an API key

Open the OpenAI API keys page, sign in, and choose Create new secret key.

[Open OpenAI API keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)

> **Screenshot needed:** OpenAI API keys page in desktop Chrome with Create new secret key clearly visible.
>
> **Solid-redact:** Organization name, Project name, Account email, Existing key names

Last verified: Pending screenshot capture.

## 2. Copy it now

Copy the new key when it appears. OpenAI only shows the complete value once.

> **Screenshot needed:** New secret key dialog with the copy control visible and the complete key covered by a solid redaction.
>
> **Solid-redact:** Complete API key, Key name, Project name, Account email

Last verified: Pending screenshot capture.

## 3. Find available models

In AI settings, enter https://api.openai.com/v1, paste the key, then choose Find models.

In Rapid Rater: **Open AI settings**

> **Screenshot needed:** Rapid Rater AI settings with the OpenAI base URL filled in and Find models highlighted.
>
> **Solid-redact:** API key field, Signed-in email

Last verified: Pending screenshot capture.

## 4. Test and save

Choose a model from the returned list, then select Test and save.

> **Screenshot needed:** Rapid Rater AI settings with a model selected and the Test and save button enabled.
>
> **Solid-redact:** API key field, Signed-in email

Last verified: Pending screenshot capture.
